m_Item1.QuestionText: One can convert a binary tree into its mirror image by traversing it in
m_Item1.Explanation:
Inorder      :  3 1 5 2 4
PreOrder   : 1 3 2 5 4
PostOrder :  3 5 4 2 1
 So we will get mirror image with postorder

m_Item1.QuestionText:
Which traversals of tree 1 and tree 2, will produce the same sequence of
node names?
m_Item1.Explanation:
In G E H I J D F B A C   G J I H E F D B C A  
Pre A B D E G H I J F C   G F E I J H C D B A  
Post G J I H E F D B C A    J H I E B D A C F G

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which among the following is not a binary search tree?
m_Item1.Explanation: In Binary Search Tree every node having value greater then root must be at right side and node having value less then root should be at left side all trees are implements according to this but only tree availabe in B option is not , 2 is less then 5 but available at right side which and it should be at left side of 5 , so tree in B option is not Binary Search Tree.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In ______________ tree, the heights of two child subtree of any node differ by at most one
m_Item1.Explanation: AVL tree is a self-balancing Binary Search Tree (BST) where the difference between heights of left and right subtrees cannot be more than one for all nodes.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The post order traversal of a binary tree is DEBFCA. Find out the pre order traversal
m_Item1.Explanation:
If We Implement Tree with above information then it will be like this


In Order        :  A B D E C F
Pre Order     :  A B D E C F  
Post Order    :  D E B F C A

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which type of traversal of binary search tree outputs the values in sorted order?
A full binary tree with 2n+1 nodes contain
m_Item1.Explanation: If we consider n as 2 then 2*n+1 is 5 .Here in given Graph we have 5 nodes where node 1 and 2 holding address of left and right children. Node 4 , 5 and 3  holding null pointer and consider as leaf nodes  So If 5 nodes are there n that is 2 non leaf nodes are available.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is a full binary tree?

Options.OptionText: Each node has exactly zero or two children
Each node has exactly two children
All the leaf can be at different level
Each node has exactly one or two children
